fix(frontend): unwrap backend API envelope in axios response interceptor

data directly. Add a response interceptor to api.ts that strips the
envelope so all useQuery hooks receive the typed payload without
per-page workarounds.

commit.message:
Claude/site overhaul cleanup z cr4u (#141)

* feat: major site overhaul — 8 new on-call protocols, 3 new decision tools, bug fixes across site

- oncall.html: fix broken protocol list (template literal bug), add 8 new protocols
  (UTI/sepsis, hyperkalemia, hypoglycemia, acute stroke, GI bleed, respiratory
  distress, syncope, acute agitation/BPSD) — now 11 total protocols
- study.html: fix duplicate HTML IDs on tabs/selects, fix broken spaced repetition
  rating (SR click handler was empty), wire all missing event listeners for tabs,
  flashcards, AI tutor, and quiz interactions
- decisions.html: add Falls Risk Assessment, FRAIL Scale (frailty screen), Braden
  Scale (pressure injury risk), plus 4 new quick reference protocols (pain mgmt,
  insomnia, AKI, DVT prophylaxis)
- index.html: update stats to dynamic loading from content.json, add Board Exam Prep
  as main tool card, add What's New banner, expand search index with 22 entries
  including topic-specific searches
- dashboard.html: wire missing Home button click handler
- exam.html: wire missing Home button click handler
- iv-protocols.html: add Home navigation link in header
- gerifellow-assistant: add Home navigation link in sidebar
- sw.js: bump cache to v5, add shared CSS/JS assets to cache list

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Da5NbNKsUTdTLUhjPhyu87

* feat: expand content.json with 8 new geriatrics board topics (585 MCQs, 296 flashcards)

New topics: Pressure Injuries, Geriatric Rehabilitation, Elder Abuse,
Vision & Hearing, Driving & Competency, Immunizations, Vascular Dementia
& DLB, Ethics & Decision Making. Each with 22-25 MCQs and 12 flashcards
in Hebrew/English mix at board-exam level. Bumps SW cache to v6.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Da5NbNKsUTdTLUhjPhyu87

* feat: integrate Toranot shift manager + audit fixes across site

- Add Toranot (toranot.netlify.app) as main tool card, quick-access link,
  clinical grid entry, and search data on index.html
- Add Shift Manager links to dashboard.html header and oncall.html header
- Fix 404.html: replace blind redirect with proper error page + nav links
- Fix manifest.json: use relative start_url, split icon purposes per spec
- Fix clinical-card styling: white text for dark gradient cards (IV, GeriFellow, Shift)
- Fix fa-sparkles icon (Pro-only) replaced with fa-bolt (free)
- Fix hardcoded platform URL in console API to use window.location
- Bump service worker cache to v7
- Update README with Toranot quick link and features section
- Update stats: 12 clinical tools, add Toranot to Recent Updates

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Da5NbNKsUTdTLUhjPhyu87

* fix: comprehensive audit fixes — XSS, accessibility, PWA, and code quality

Security:
- Fix stored XSS in ai-assistant.html chat history (escape item.query in innerHTML)
- Add missing x-api-key header to study.html Anthropic API call
- Use SecureStorage with 24h TTL for API key in ai-assistant.html
- Add CSP meta tag to 404.html
- Consolidate esc() into shared/sanitize.js, remove 4 inline duplicates

Accessibility:
- Remove user-scalable=no from iv-protocols.html and szmc-presentation-maker
- Add ARIA role="tablist" and role="tab" to assessments.html tab navigation
- Add skip-to-main-content link to index.html
- Add id="main-content" landmark to index.html <main>

Bugs:
- Fix duplicate HTML IDs on tab buttons in assessments.html
- Fix switchTab selector mismatch in gerifellow-assistant (used :first-child but first child is Home link)
- Add clipboard API error handling with execCommand fallback in gerifellow-assistant
- Fix service worker offline fallback to serve offline.html instead of index.html

PWA:
- Add 192x192 and 512x512 PNG icons for Chrome installability
- Update manifest.json with raster icon entries

Performance:
- Add preconnect hints for fonts.googleapis.com, fonts.gstatic.com, cdnjs.cloudflare.com
- Remove duplicate viewport meta tag from szmc-presentation-maker

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Da5NbNKsUTdTLUhjPhyu87

commit.message:
feat(aiwm): add structured channels[] config for Discord/Telegram agents

Replace flat settings.discord_* / settings.telegram_* keys with a typed
channels[] array on the Agent schema. Each entry represents one channel
with granular per-channel config: platform, token, botId, channelId,
requireMentions, verboseLogging, verboseLoggingTarget.

- agent.schema.ts: add ChannelConfig interface and channels[] Prop
- agent.dto.ts: add ChannelConfigDto with validation; update
  CreateAgentDto, UpdateAgentDto, AgentConnectResponseDto
- agent.service.ts: buildEnvConfig() prioritises channels[], falls back
  to legacy settings keys; connect() returns channels in response
- README + AGENT-SETTINGS-STRUCTURE.md: update docs to reflect new
  schema, deprecate legacy keys, add migration notes

Legacy settings.discord_* / settings.telegram_* keys remain supported
for backward compatibility until all agents are fully migrated.

commit.message:
fix: V0.9.5 — Memory search bug fix (threshold applied to RRF scores)

Root cause: HybridRetriever applied similarity_threshold (0.65) to
RRF-merged scores (max ~0.033), causing 100% of results to be filtered.
Threshold now applied to cosine similarity scores before RRF merge.

Secondary fix: embedder failures (e.g. missing OPENAI_API_KEY) now fall
back to BM25-only search with a warning instead of crashing.

- hybrid-retriever.ts: pre-filter vector results by cosine threshold,
  remove threshold filter from merged RRF output, wrap embed() in try/catch
- hybrid-retriever.test.ts: add ZeroQueryEmbedder + FailingEmbedder mocks,
  update threshold test and add BM25-fallback test
- server.test.ts: update version assertion 0.9.4 → 0.9.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add privacy section to README

no analytics, no telemetry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add GPU Lesson 32 — Skinning Animations (#170) (#171)

* Add GPU Lesson 32 — Skinning Animations

Introduces vertex skinning with glTF joint hierarchies. CesiumMan (19
joints, 57 animation channels, 3273 skinned vertices) walks in a loop
with deforming shadows.

New concepts covered:
- Skinning equation: jointMatrix = inverse(meshWorld) * jointWorld * IBM
- Per-vertex blend weights (4 joints per vertex, USHORT4 + FLOAT4)
- Dynamic animation channel parsing from glTF JSON
- Animated shadow maps with skinned vertex shaders

Files added:
- lessons/gpu/32-skinning-animations/ — lesson code, shaders, README
- .claude/skills/forge-skinning-animations/SKILL.md — reusable skill

Files modified:
- common/gltf/forge_gltf.h — skin parsing (joints, inverse bind matrices)
- tests/gltf/test_gltf.c — CesiumMan skin tests
- scripts/forge_diagrams/ — joint matrix pipeline diagram
- CMakeLists.txt, README.md, PLAN.md — project integration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR #171 feedback: move CesiumMan to shared assets, harden parsing

- Move CesiumMan model to assets/models/ (shared location, no duplication)
- Accept UNSIGNED_BYTE joint indices per glTF 2.0 spec (widen to Uint16)
- Use size_t for skin allocation arithmetic to prevent overflow
- Validate node/skin/joint indices against array bounds
- Set identity matrices when IBM accessor is invalid or short
- Validate animation keyframe counts and buffer bounds before use
- Fix world-transform rebuild to resolve parents before children
- Gate mouse_captured state on SDL_SetWindowRelativeMouseMode success
- Check SDL_WaitForGPUIdle return value during shutdown
- Add bounds check before scene.nodes[2] in test
- Use FORGE_GLTF_PATH_SIZE constant in test path buffer
- Add "Key concepts" section heading to README
- Fix diagram xlim to contain rightmost box
- Shorten diagram docstring

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR #171 round-2 feedback: accessor validation, index safety

- Fix SDL_Log format argument (ch → i) to prevent UB
- Validate output accessor count, componentType, and type before
  accepting animation channels
- Validate skin/joint/skeleton indices against parsed scene counts
  instead of compile-time MAX constants
- Use -1 sentinel for invalid joint indices instead of coercing to 0
- Defer node.skin_index validation until after skins are parsed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix joint matrix pipeline diagram layout

Widen figure and distribute boxes evenly so the rightmost box is not
cut off and spacing between all four boxes is uniform.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: v2.57.15 — Phase 6 falling-knife filter for HV Cluster strategy

Train 2016-2023 (23,140 signals, 35.1% base falling-knife rate).
Two filter variants:
  A. Rules-filter  — 4 NEWGEN-derived gates (vol_multiple, OFI, ichimoku, ParSAR)
  B. Scored-filter — 23-feature bearish vote, block at score ≥ 15

Result: Neither filter improves val CAGR over unfiltered baseline.
  Unfiltered val (2024-2026): +16.7%  Rules: +14.8%  Scored: +9.8%
Key insight: the 4 NEWGEN bearish signals co-occur on bounces more than
falling knives in the broader universe — NEWGEN was stock-specific.
Scored filter hurts 2025 (-12.1% vs +31.5%) by blocking too many winners.

Verdict: Filter NOT RECOMMENDED for deployment. NEWGEN's bearish pattern
was idiosyncratic (post-earnings fundamental shock), not a generalised
predictor of HV Cluster failures across 500 stocks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Three Pillars document

- Result<T, E> as fundamental building block (not just a monad)
- Option<T> for explicit absence (not nullable)
- Unit for explicit completion (not void)
- Why these are necessities, not functional patterns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(btc): trade archive system — never lose historical trade data again

New module: tradeArchive.js
- archiveTrades(version, config, notes) — moves trades to trade_archive table
- getConfigVersions() — list all config versions with stats
- getArchivedTrades(version) — get trades for a specific version

API endpoints:
- POST /api/btc/archive { version, notes } — archive + wipe trades
- GET /api/btc/archive/versions — list versions with WR/PnL/PF
- GET /api/btc/archive/trades/:version — get trades for a version

Each archive captures:
- Full config snapshot (JSON)
- All trade data preserved
- Summary stats: WR, PnL, PF, direction accuracy, exit reasons
- Start/end timestamps

Requires: run archive-schema.sql in Supabase SQL Editor first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix dungeon detection, tighten snow region to D1 corridor (v33-v34)

- Fix dungeon_index >= 1 false positive: Hero's Cave (0xFF) passed the
  check because 255 >= 1. Changed to 1 <= dungeon_index < 0xFF in all
  4 detection sites (reward_wrapper.py, phase_rewards.py).

- Add SNOW_REGION_MIN_ROW/MIN_COL bounds to restrict snow region from
  entire NW quadrant (~108 rooms) to D1 corridor (rows 9-11, cols 6-7,
  6 rooms). Prevents agent from earning +2000 bonus by going west toward
  Hero's Cave instead of north toward D1.

- Add dungeon metrics to training milestones and phase detection.
- Add zelda_env.py state_encoder improvements.
- Add 0xFF regression test to test_phase_rewards.py.
- Align phase_detector.py with phase_rewards.py phases.

commit.message:
refactor: remove dead Python CLI, reports, ratings, and bloat modules

Spring cleanup of the Python subproject — remove ~14,200 lines of unused
code to keep the package focused as a functional gym + ML training toolkit.

Removed modules:
- cli/ (Click CLI wrapper, never used — scripts called directly)
- analysis/report/ (HTML report generator, 21 files)
- analysis/visualize.py, parse_log.py, performance_dashboard.py
- ratings/ (ELO tracking, 4 files)
- models/ (registry + discovery)
- infra/ (experiment tracking, predates W&B)
- viz/ (ASCII renderer, never imported)
- hub.py (HuggingFace integration, used once)
- agents/card2vec.py, agents/decision_transformer.py (superseded)

Also removed: associated scripts, tests, docs, data files, and the
click dependency. Fixed cascading references in eval/unified.py
(inlined simple ELO), training/callbacks.py, __init__.py, pyproject.toml.

All 814 Rust tests and 158 Python tests pass.

commit.message:
Add global blocking default, in-page toast indicator, and timed allow

- Enable blocking globally by default on install; all sites blocked
  without any setup, with per-site exceptions (allowlist) instead of
  per-site opt-in
- In-page shadow DOM toast shows blocked count with bump animation on
  each intercept; auto-dismisses after 5 s
- "Allow site" expands to duration picker: 5 min, 1 hr, or Forever
- Timed exceptions stored as allowedUntil timestamp, checked lazily in
  shouldBlock; expired exceptions cleaned on startup and every 60 s
- Popup exception list shows remaining time (e.g. "42m left")
- Background sends POPUP_BLOCKED to source tab for nav-intercepted popups
- Fix: use chrome.extension.inIncognitoContext instead of
  chrome.windows.getCurrent (not available in content scripts)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
